revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image for a game-development pixel-art asset.

Canvas and sheet contract:
- Exact image size: 64x64 pixels.
- Grid: 4 rows x 4 columns.
- Frame size: 16x16 pixels.
- 16 frames total.
- No visible grid lines, no labels, no borders, no tile IDs, no text, no watermark, no UI.
- Every frame must stay inside its own 16x16 cell and keep the same scale.
- All 16 cells filled edge-to-edge and fully opaque where tile art exists.
- Use solid #FF00FF magenta only for any truly empty space, but this terrain sheet should be effectively filled by opaque tile art.
- Crisp low-resolution SNES-style pixel art, no antialiasing blur, readable pixel clusters, consistent palette, dark one-pixel outline where useful, top-left light source.

Asset profile:
- Profile: snes16_platformer_v1.
- Preset: world_forest_mass_16.
- Asset kind: terrain-tile.
- Asset type: tile.
- Subject: top-down sacred grove canopy / chinju no mori forest mass autotile for a world map.

Overall style:
- Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette.
- Soft warm golden-hour sunlight.
- Hand-painted Ghibli-like mood, but original and non-branded.
- Rounded broadleaf tree crowns: camphor, evergreen oak, chinquapin.
- Deep rich emerald and jade greens.
- Dappled sunlit highlights on top leaf pads.
- Deep cool blue-green shadow pooling under interior canopies.
- Individual tree crowns must read as large rounded leaf clusters, not a flat solid green mass.
- Edge crowns are lower, smaller, and lighter than interior crowns.
- The forest must read as a large geographic region with organic outline, not rows of identical trees and not close-up village ground detail.
- Avoid visible checkerboard repetition: vary texture, crown placement, edge breakup, and highlight positions while keeping the set cohesive and seamlessly usable.

Strict 4x4 layout, left to right per row:
Row 1, CENTER variations, cells 0-3:
1. Full-density interior canopy variation A: closely packed rounded crowns, deep saturated greens, dappled top-left highlights, cool shadow between crowns.
2. Full-density interior canopy variation B: same density and scale, shifted crown arrangement and dapple positions.
3. Full-density interior canopy variation C: more dark interior pooling, still bright top highlights.
4. Full-density interior canopy variation D: slightly different broadleaf crown pattern to prevent tiled repetition.

Row 2, DIRECTIONAL EDGES, cells 4-7, order N, S, W, E:
5. North edge: forest-to-grass boundary along the north side; open grass outside the forest at the top. The boundary is wavy/scalloped, never straight. Include three-point vertical depth: outer small bright lower shrub fringe at the boundary, tall dark trunks/deep shadow behind it, soft cast shadow fading onto the grass outside.
6. South edge: forest-to-grass boundary along the south side; open grass outside the forest at the bottom. Wavy/scalloped line, small bright fringe, dark trunk-shadow depth, soft cast shadow on grass outside.
7. West edge: forest-to-grass boundary along the west side; open grass outside the forest at the left. Wavy/scalloped line with uneven crown bulges protruding into grass, bright lower shrubs, dark trunk-shadow depth, soft cast shadow on grass.
8. East edge: forest-to-grass boundary along the east side; open grass outside the forest at the right. Wavy/scalloped line, bright lower shrubs, dark trunk-shadow depth, soft cast shadow on grass.

Row 3, OUTER CORNERS, cells 8-11, order NW, NE, SW, SE:
9. Northwest outer corner: organically rounded forest promontory with grass outside north and west, curved scalloped shrub fringe, partial crown bulges, no 90-degree hard corner.
10. Northeast outer corner: organically rounded forest promontory with grass outside north and east, curved scalloped shrub fringe, partial crown bulges, no hard corner.
11. Southwest outer corner: organically rounded forest promontory with grass outside south and west, curved scalloped shrub fringe, partial crown bulges, no hard corner.
12. Southeast outer corner: organically rounded forest promontory with grass outside south and east, curved scalloped shrub fringe, partial crown bulges, no hard corner.

Row 4, ACCENT / TRANSITION, cells 12-15:
13. Thin one-cell forest strip: small isolated tree/bush hedge strip, forest thinner than a single interior cell, still top-down broadleaf canopy and usable as a narrow grove strip.
14. Forest-edge low ground-level bush transition: fern, moss, and low-light plants under the grove edge; grass blends naturally into small shrubs and roots.
15. Forest shadow edge accent: mostly cast shadow on open grass with a few dark trunks and tiny shrub silhouettes, usable as an outermost shadow overlay.
16. Forest inner-corner concave tile: open clearing bites into the forest; interior forest wraps around three sides with a small organic grassy gap on one side, scalloped concave edge, no square cutout.

Tile quality and terrain transition requirements:
- Forest edges must use soft graduated non-rectangular transitions.
- Eliminate the square forest problem: boundaries should be organic, wavy, scalloped, and crown-based.
- Blend grass, dirt hints, moss, shrub fringe, trunk-shadow, and canopy without hard rectangular bands.
- Include chipped edge breakup, grass overlap, lower fringe shrubs, and decoration variants where appropriate.
- Use broad color drift in grass and low-frequency texture, not repeated grass stamps.
- Maintain consistent identity and scale across all frames.

Negative constraints:
- No isometric perspective.
- No characters, no birds, no companion creatures.
- No samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects.
- No logos, no letter emblems, no branded likenesses, no modern text.
- No horror mood, no dark gloomy palette.
- Do not imitate or include any Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, or Sakuna characters, logos, symbols, or likenesses.

Output requirement: exactly one crisp high-resolution display PNG of the 64x64 pixel-art sprite sheet, with the pixel grid implied by the art only and no drawn grid lines.
